progressive_sections.body: Current status is local_aggregate_ready for dataset revision main.
progressive_sections.heading: Scope

progressive_sections.body: The largest aggregate unit by law-record count is los_angeles_county (CA, county) with 11503 records.
progressive_sections.heading: Largest visible aggregate units

progressive_sections.body: The briefing is derived from map_layers.json and charts.json. It omits raw text, headers, and source locators.
progressive_sections.heading: Evidence boundary

briefings.question: What does the real-data Pages layer currently cover?
briefings.short_answer: The public layer represents 1000 aggregate jurisdiction units, 1517672 LOCUS law records, and 50 states or district entries. It publishes aggregate counts only, not ordinance text.
briefings.title: Published aggregate coverage

progressive_sections.body: 177 county polygons and 815 municipal/town points are machine matched to the aggregate LOCUS layer pending review.
progressive_sections.heading: Current geography

rows.meaning: dominant LOCUS topic in aggregate counts
dominant LOCUS function in aggregate counts

progressive_sections.body: County geometry status: official_census_county_geometry_machine_matched_pending_review. Municipal geometry status: official_census_municipal_points_machine_matched_pending_review. State-cluster fallback status: state_clustered_approximate_layout_until_reviewed_county_town_geometries_available.
progressive_sections.heading: Geometry provenance

briefings.question: How should I read the county and town colored map?
briefings.short_answer: The map supports aggregate color modes for neutral tier, dominant topic, dominant function, and law-count intensity. Colors are exploratory model-output summaries, not rankings.
briefings.title: County and town map coloring

progressive_sections.body: Topics, functions, score dimensions, neutral tiers, and jurisdiction units are represented.
progressive_sections.heading: Core node types

progressive_sections.body: Top aggregate topic: Other (407704). Top aggregate function: Rules (962831).
progressive_sections.heading: Dominant aggregate signals

progressive_sections.body: Ontology edges summarize model-produced dataset fields. They are not verified legal relationships.
progressive_sections.heading: Interpretation boundary

briefings.question: What ontology is being built from LOCUS?
briefings.short_answer: The current ontology has 1018 nodes and 3000 edges linking jurisdiction units to released topics, functions, neutral tiers, and score dimensions.
briefings.title: Ontology from released LOCUS fields

progressive_sections.body: The browser imports aggregate outputs for substantivity, function, topic, and four continuous score fields.
progressive_sections.heading: Released outputs

progressive_sections.body: Do not interpret a higher or lower score as more or less lawful, burdensome, free, opaque, or paternalistic until source model documentation is reviewed.
progressive_sections.heading: No legal conclusion

briefings.question: What do the model scores mean on this site?
briefings.short_answer: The site displays released LOCUS labels and neutral score aggregates. Score direction is not verified here.
briefings.title: Model-output interpretation

progressive_sections.body: Grok API keys must never be embedded in GitHub Pages JavaScript.
progressive_sections.heading: Browser policy

progressive_sections.body: A GitHub Actions or local Python job may use GROK_API_KEY to generate static aggregate-only summaries.
progressive_sections.heading: Offline generation

progressive_sections.body: The generated public artifact must not contain raw ordinance text, headers, source locators, secrets, or private local paths.
progressive_sections.heading: Data boundary

briefings.question: Can Grok answer live questions on the GitHub Pages site?
briefings.short_answer: No. GitHub Pages is static. Grok can only enrich aggregate artifacts in offline jobs, never in browser JavaScript.
briefings.title: Publication and Grok boundary

citation: Denis Peskoff, Joe Barrow, Christopher Vu, and Diag Davenport. "Freeing the Law with LOCUS: A Local Ordinance Corpus for the United States." arXiv:2606.19334, 2026.
grok_summary: The EvoLOCUS aggregate covers 1,517,672 laws across 1,000 units (823 cities, 177 counties) in 50 states, drawn from the main revision. An ontology of 1,018 nodes and 3,000 edges is supplied, with Rules and Other the leading categories. Raw ordinance text is not published. Model-produced scores remain neutral unless their direction is independently verified; tier distribution is balanced at the unit level.

publication_policy.notice: Briefings summarize aggregate artifacts for research review. They are not legal advice or legal findings.

suggested_questions: What does the real-data Pages layer currently cover?
How should I read the county and town colored map?
What ontology is being built from LOCUS?
What do the model scores mean on this site?
